root_text.an4.158:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an4.158:1.3: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato sāriputtassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an4.158:2.1: “Yo hi koci, āvuso, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā cattāro dhamme attani samanupassati, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.158:2.5: Rāgavepullattaṁ, dosavepullattaṁ, mohavepullattaṁ, gambhīresu kho panassa ṭhānāṭhānesu paññācakkhu na kamati.
root_text.an4.158:2.6: Yo hi koci, āvuso, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā ime cattāro dhamme attani samanupassati, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.158:3.1: Yo hi koci, āvuso, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā cattāro dhamme attani samanupassati, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.158:3.5: Rāgatanuttaṁ, dosatanuttaṁ, mohatanuttaṁ, gambhīresu kho panassa ṭhānāṭhānesu paññācakkhu kamati.
root_text.an4.158:3.6: Yo hi koci, āvuso, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā ime cattāro dhamme attani samanupassati, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:

translation_text.an4.158:2.1: “Reverends, any monk or nun who sees four things inside themselves should conclude:
translation_text.an4.158:2.3: For this is what the Buddha calls decline.’
translation_text.an4.158:2.5: They have much greed, much hate, and much delusion; and their wisdom eye doesn’t go into the many deep matters.
translation_text.an4.158:2.6: Any monk or nun who sees these four things inside themselves should conclude:
translation_text.an4.158:2.8: For this is what the Buddha calls decline.’
translation_text.an4.158:3.1: Any monk or nun who sees four things inside themselves should conclude:
translation_text.an4.158:3.3: For this is what the Buddha calls non-decline.’
translation_text.an4.158:3.5: Their greed, hate, and delusion grow less; and their wisdom eye goes into the many deep matters.
translation_text.an4.158:3.6: Any monk or nun who sees these four things inside themselves should conclude:
translation_text.an4.158:3.8: For this is what the Buddha calls non-decline.’”
